steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The phrase “too delicate to be trodden upon by rough feet” reveals what “delicate” means — something easily damaged or harmed by being stepped on.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
The underlined word needs to mean easily damaged or ruined by rough contact — the carpet looks too precious to step on. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — Generous: "too generous to be trodden upon" — generous means freely giving, which has nothing to do with being harmed by footsteps. ✗
  • Choice B — Fragile: "too fragile to be trodden upon" — fragile means easily broken or damaged by force, which fits perfectly: the snowy carpet looks too breakable for rough feet. ✓
  • Choice C — Accurate: "too accurate to be trodden upon" — accurate means precise or correct, which makes no sense for a carpet being stepped on. ✗
  • Choice D — Empty: "too empty to be trodden upon" — empty means containing nothing, which doesn't explain why you'd avoid stepping on it. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
